full_response:
Let Uμ(τ)=(U⁰,U¹,U²,U³)=(cosh(ατ), sinh(ατ), 0, 0).  In Cartesian Minkowski coords all Γρ
μν=0 so the geodesic eqn Uᵃ∇ₐUᵇ=0 reduces to   
  
 Uᵃ∂ₐUᵇ=0.  
  
But along the world‐line d/dτ=Uᵃ∂ₐ, so  
  
 Uᵃ∂ₐUᵇ= dUᵇ/dτ.  
  
Compute the derivative:  
  
 dU⁰/dτ = α sinh(ατ),  
 dU¹/dτ = α cosh(ατ),  
 dU²/dτ = dU³/dτ = 0.  
  
Hence the 4‐acceleration Aᵇ≡dUᵇ/dτ=(α sinhατ, α coshατ, 0, 0)≠0.  Therefore  
  
 Uᵃ∇ₐUᵇ = Aᵇ ≠ 0,  
  
so the world‐line is not a geodesic.  
  
\boxed{U^a\nabla_a U^b=\frac{dU^b}{d\tau}=(\alpha\sinh(\alpha\tau),\,\alpha\cosh(\alpha\tau),\,0,\,0)\neq0\quad\Longrightarrow\text{not a geodesic}}  
  
Confidence: 100%
